ConstructorArguments: Get a temporary render target. You should dispose the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.

Documentation.Summary: Get a temporary render target. You should dispose the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.
Documentation.Return: A RenderTarget that is ready to render to.

Params.width: Width of the render target you want.
Params.height: Height of the render target you want.
Params.colorFormat: The format for the color buffer. If set to default we'll use whatever the current pipeline is using.
Params.depthFormat: The format for the depth buffer.
Params.msaa: The number of msaa samples you'd like. Msaa render textures are a pain in the ass so you're probably gonna regret trying to use this.
Params.numMips: Number of mips you want in this texture. You probably don't want this unless you want to generate mips in a second pass.
Params.targetName: The optional name of the render target

ConstructorArguments: Get a temporary render target. You should dispose the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.

Documentation.Summary: Get a temporary render target. You should dispose the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.
Documentation.Return: A RenderTarget that is ready to render to.

Params.sizeFactor: Divide the screen size by this factor. 2 would be half screen sized. 1 for full screen sized.
Params.colorFormat: The format for the color buffer. If null we'll choose the most appropriate for where you are in the pipeline.
Params.depthFormat: The format for the depth buffer.
Params.msaa: The number of msaa samples you'd like. Msaa render textures are a pain in the ass so you're probably gonna regret trying to use this.
Params.numMips: Number of mips you want in this texture. You probably don't want this unless you want to generate mips in a second pass.
Params.targetName: The optional name of the render target

ConstructorArguments: Translucent pass. We're rendering translucent objects in depth sorted order, from back to front.

ConstructorArguments: Create a texture from the scope. The texture will either be a cached version or will be rendered immediately

Documentation.Summary: Create a texture from the scope. The texture will either be a cached version or will be rendered immediately

ConstructorArguments: Measures the rendered size of the text in this Sandbox.TextRendering.Scope using its current style settings. This is non trivial but the underlying style is cached, so if you end up drawing it, it'll re-use the cached data anyway.

Documentation.Summary:
Measures the rendered size of the text in this <see cref="T:Sandbox.TextRendering.Scope" /> using its current style settings. This is non trivial
but the underlying style is cached, so if you end up drawing it, it'll re-use the cached data anyway.
Documentation.Return: A <see cref="T:Vector2" /> representing the width and height, in pixels, of the rendered text.

Documentation.Summary: Defines a scope of text, all using the same style.

ConstructorArguments: Allows for the definition of custom vertex layouts

Documentation.Summary: Allows for the definition of custom vertex layouts
